target.name: Newcleo
target.founder: Stefano Buono, Luciano Cinotti, Elisabeth Rizzotti

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $755M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Newcleo, a Series B nuclear energy company that has raised $755M+, is valued within a fragmented frontier-tech sector comprising 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors including Type One Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, and X-Energy. The private comparables universe remains sparse and heterogeneous in stage and capital structure, limiting multi-method triangulation.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $755M raised proxy, reflecting typical Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $1.4B. Peer-median valuation is not applicable; although 8 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with aligned valuation tiers produces fewer than three datapoints, making a narrow peer set unreliable. A broad sector median would conflate companies at materially different development and funding stages.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.2B (low) to $1.6B (high), centered on $1.4B. This range is anchored to a single methodological input, making it sensitive to the step-up multiplier assumption and the validity of total raised as a pre-Series B valuation proxy. Key uncertainties include (1) the absence of sufficient near-peer transactions to validate the 1.9x multiplier for this specific subsector, (2) the stage and capital efficiency of the selected private comparables, and (3) the timing and terms of any interim financing rounds post-Series A.
